comparison: gemma4:26b vs gemma4:31b-cloud on CREST v1.3 Judgment Benchmark
run_by: Forge (subagent, dispatched by Yoda per CREST Execute routing)
approval_context: Ken approved ad-hoc comparison via NEEDS-KEN-CREST-BENCHMARK-26B
scope_notes: Both runs use the existing think:false injection (TKT-1017/CHG-0956). Script defaults were NOT changed. OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL unchanged at 1. No models removed. openclaw.json and vendor code NOT touched.

baseline.mean_eval_tokens_per_sec_note: Not measured by current script. Per-call ollama server-side duration captured as proxy (sum of all /api/generate response times from ~/.ollama/logs/server.log in run window).

candidate.mean_eval_tokens_per_sec_note: Not measured by current script. Per-call ollama server-side duration captured as proxy (sum of all /api/generate response times from ~/.ollama/logs/server.log in run window).

comparison_summary.interpretation: Both models achieve a perfect 20/20 on the CREST v1.3 judgment benchmark with think:false injection. gemma4:26b is ~5x faster wall-clock and substantially more consistent (jitter 1.98s vs 31.23s). 26b is local-only (no cloud dependency, no network egress, no per-token cost). 31b-cloud is gated on Ollama cloud availability and showed wide latency variance in this run (up to 31.9s single-call).

verdict_reasoning: 26b matches 31b benchmark score (20/20 vs 20/20); 26b also clears the >=90% threshold. Update script default to gemma4:26b. Note: this is a RECOMMENDATION only. The committed default in scripts/crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh was NOT changed per the task's explicit safety rule. Awaiting Ken's follow-up approval before any default swap.
verdict_line_for_ken: SWAP — 26b matches 31b benchmark score (20/20 vs 20/20); update script default to gemma4:26b
actions_pending_ken_approval: scripts/crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh: change default MODEL line from 'gemma4:31b-cloud' to 'gemma4:26b' (NEEDS KEN APPROVAL before execution)
scripts/crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh: update header comment to note new default and 20/20 evidence
